channel.title: Comments for page "Snip Snip Snip"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Snip Snip Snip"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this comment is old, but for posterity if other people read your comment, I want to mention that you probably got the previous Clipper and this Clipper confused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A few really good lines, but overall I've seen this all before. Doesn't work for me. And I don't like the 1981 connection, though that's more a nitpick that didn't affect my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Strictly speaking, 'sociopath' and 'psychopath' are synonymous. But I understand what you meant by that line anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the first time I recall feeling that AWCY? was scary. They are always bizarre and frequently differently-saned; but even their most gruesome/lethal pieces usually come across to me as merely depravedly indifferent to human life. With The Clipper 2.0, they will definitely cross the line from sociopath to balls out psychopath.

I wonder how the AWCY? current membership will feel about his freedom of artistic expression when he demonstrates that he was speaking literally that whole time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, you're right, my bad. I still don't see any real connection between this guy and that video. Nor do I want to see that connection, as it makes 1981 a lot less creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eugh.

Take my upvote. Continue this series. Please. I don't mind the Reagan thing either, BTW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He wasn't AWCY? when he made that, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I have to echo what others have said about 1981. At first I thought "cool!", but after awhile it really started to bug me, because 1981 doesn't seem like anything AWCY? would do at all. I really think it detracts from the quality of the tale, though I still liked it. I still think the 173 link in one of your previous articles works, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't think of a character that can't be shoehorned into one TVTropes page or another.

True, but there's a difference between a character being shoehorned as a trope and a character epitomizing that trope. And yeah, there's nothing wrong with using tropes (I actually really like Mad Artist as a trope), it's just that this uses the trope and adds nothing to it.1 Vaguely Ed Gein-ish dude talking about art isn't a super-new thing. It provides characterization, sure; it's just dull characterization.

Footnotes
1. That I can see. At present

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't think of a character that can't be shoehorned into one TVTropes page or another. That he immediately pulled the trope into your mind provided rapid characterisation, and as such, fitting the trope served its purpose. If there was another character on the site that fit that trope, I'd have readily used them, thus removing the need for this tale at all, but there wasn't, so I created one. Unfortunately, characterisation pieces are hardly thrilling, but they're a necessary evil. I'm glad that you think this is better than Ruiz. I get why you think he's flat, but this was only the starting point of a character that I'm planning to elsewhere. Don't take this as me going 'bluh bluh I am justified in establishing cardboard cutouts', your critiques on these stories are telling me where I can improve, so thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's only art if someone thinks it is.

Ergo, if it exists, it's art.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the Reagan thing

Fuck me, this was art?

Creepy as all get-out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't like the implication that this guy made SCP-1981, I always thought of that being something more otherworldly than a mere human-created anomaly. I know all SCPs are supernatural by definition, but there's supernatural and supernatural, y'know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly think you could do without the 1981 link, but other than that this entertained me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite the head-on collision with my headcanon (The Hanged Prince sees you), this is a nice tale. The speech at the end really made it come together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tie in with 1981 was at first intriguing, but is steadily making me more annoyed. 1981 was awesome because of how inexplicably inhuman the antagonist is. FFS, it lived in the video tape!

Despite my gripe, and the truth that this idea is run a little thin, the writing is very good.

So 0 for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I am enjoying this canon and all the characters. HOWEVER, while I _sort_ of enjoyed the Reagan connection, I think SCP-1981 is just too big and awesome to be a simple AWCY? piece. In my headcanon, 1981 will always be some demonic lovecraftian possession horror-spill into our universe. Although I do admit the title "RONALD REAGAN CUT UP WHILE TALKING" certainly sounds like an art piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally can't see this guy making SCP-1981, but I still enjoyed this enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been waiting for someone to write a tale about a psychopathic serial killer, and now it's finally here. Not only is it about a psychopathic killer, it's about an AWCY? killer. And that's awesome. Also:

that thing back in '88, the Reagan thing

Fuck. Yes.
+1 with the force of a thousand suns

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I do what other people only dream. I make art until someone dies. (giggles) I am the world's first fully-functioning homicidal artist. "

Hey wow a pithy summary of this tale as well as what I dislike about it. Craaaaaaazy guy makes art that involves killing people isn't a new thing and this doesn't really bring anything else to the table.

A better story than yet another Ruiz Duchamp thing doesn't equate to a good story. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is gross and great in equal measure. Great wins the tie.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this origin story for The Clipper. I'm not sure how I feel about the differences between his voice here and in "A Cooler Manifesto", but I do like the before-and-after effect you've made with the two stories.

The way you've written The Clipper here makes

the Reagan thing

seem right up his alley, in my opinion. Final note: quality body horror. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is disgustingly absurd in all the best ways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have some more love in the form of an upvote, friend. And keep away from my vitreous humors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm scared of the Clipper. I mean,

the Reagan thing

This alone would be enough. But then there's the rest of this guy's personality, and deaugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Metacommentary.
